text:
Athletes competing in the2024 Olympic Games in Pariscould be set for a hefty payday if they secure gold. The summer sporting spectacle commences on Friday, July 26, with a plethora of competitors vying to etch their names into history as the best in their respective disciplines.

But it's not just glory on the line over the coming weeks, there's also a substantial prize pot available for the top performers. Although theInternational Olympic Committeedoesn't provide prize money to participants or medal winners,National Olympic Committeesoften reward their triumphant athletes.

World Athleticshas revealed that they will be awarding £38,700 in prize money to gold medallists in track and field events for the first time ever, while theInternational Boxing Association (IBA)has declared a whopping £2.4million payout to this summer's medallists. Gold medal-winning boxers are set to receive £38,700, according to reports, while those securing silver will receive £19,300 and bronze medallists will pocket £9,600.

The payouts from National Committees vary based on location. For instance, Australia's Medal Incentive Funding Programme typically awards athletes £10,137 ($20,000) for gold, £7,603 ($15,000) for silver, and £5,067 ($10,000) for bronze, whereas the USA reportedly pays out respective amounts of £29,103 ($37,500), £17,462 ($22,500) and £11,644 ($15,000), reportsthe Mirror.

In a stark contrast to Team GB athletes, who reportedly receive no financial reward for securing a podium finish at the Olympic Games, Singapore has announced it would award its athletes up to £776,000 ($1million) for winning gold at the Tokyo Olympics 2021. Athletes from New Zealand, Norway and Sweden will also receive nothing for a podium placement.

However, National Olympic Committees' prize pots are not the only source of income for Olympians. Lucrative sponsorship and endorsement deals can also provide substantial earnings. Additionally, some athletes maintain full-time jobs while pursuing their Olympic dreams ahead of the Paris 2024 Games.



For instance, Georgia Bell, a runner for Team GB, works in cyber security throughout the year. Kurtis Imrie, an Olympic canoeist from New Zealand, is primarily an electrician, while American boxer Morelle McCane has reportedly worked as a birthday party clown in the past.

Conversely, several successful Olympians earn enough from their sporting careers to focus on their discipline full-time. This summer, world number one golfer Scottie Scheffler will represent Team USA, whileRory McIlroywill compete for Ireland. In football,Manchester City's star Julian Alvarez will play for his native Argentina, and Lyon striker Alexandre Lacazette will represent France - both already having established careers with their respective clubs and countries.

title: How much Olympic athletes are paid at Paris 2024 after rule change | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Aaron Morris
description: The 2024 Olympic Games in Paris are almost upon us and there will be a whole host of athletes competing for money as well as medals over the coming weeks.
